# Terms of Service

_UK GDPR required document template. Replace all [placeholders] with your organisation details before publication._

**Source:** `External - Customer-Facing Documents/Terms of Service.docx`

Terms of Service

## Uk Gdpr

Agreement to These Terms

Contract

These Terms of Service ("Terms") are a legal agreement between you and [Company Name] ("[Company Name]", "we", "us") governing access to and use of the [Product Name] platform ("[Product Name]", the "Service").

By creating an account, clicking "I agree", or using [Product Name], you confirm that you accept these Terms. If you do not agree, you must not use the Service.

If you use [Product Name] on behalf of an organisation, you represent that you have authority to bind that organisation, and "you" includes that organisation.

Related documents

### These Terms incorporate by reference (where applicable)

[Product Name] Privacy Policy

[Product Name] Cookie Policy

Data Processing Agreement (for business customers where [Company Name] acts as processor)

Order form, statement of work, or online subscription plan ("Order")

If there is a conflict between these Terms and a signed Order, the Order prevails for commercial terms only; data protection terms in the DPA prevail for processing of personal data.

Territory

[Product Name] is offered exclusively in the United Kingdom (England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland). You must not use [Product Name] if you are outside the UK unless we expressly agree in writing.

The Service is hosted in Microsoft Azure UK South. We do not offer multi-region residency or non-UK deployment.

The Service

Description

[Product Name] is a cloud workspace and productivity platform providing, among other features: personal and team work boards, notes, search, collaboration, administration tools, and optional AI-assisted functions (e.g. summarisation, speech-to-text, entity suggestions).

Features may change over time. We may add, modify, or withdraw features with reasonable notice where practicable.

Non-clinical use

[Product Name] is not a medical device, clinical information system, or regulated health record. It does not provide diagnosis, treatment, clinical decision support, or emergency services.

You must not use [Product Name] as a substitute for professional medical, legal, or other regulated advice. AI-generated content is assistive only and may be inaccurate — you are responsible for reviewing output before use.

Availability

We aim for high availability but do not guarantee uninterrupted access. Maintenance, upgrades, and events beyond our reasonable control may cause downtime.

Support levels and service credits (if any) are defined in your Order or support policy.

Accounts and Eligibility

Registration

You must provide accurate registration information and keep it up to date.

Authentication is provided via Azure AD B2C or other identity methods we specify. You are responsible for safeguarding credentials and for all activity under your account.

Notify us immediately at support@[company-domain] if you suspect unauthorised access.

Eligibility

You must be at least 18 years old and capable of entering into a binding contract.

Use by organisations must comply with their internal policies and applicable law.

Administrators

Organisation administrators may manage users, permissions, and settings. You are responsible for how your organisation configures [Product Name] and for instructions given to us as processor under the DPA.

Subscriptions and Payment

Fees

Fees, billing cycle, and payment method are specified in your Order or checkout flow.

Unless stated otherwise, fees are exclusive of VAT and other taxes, which you pay where applicable.

We may change standard pricing on renewal with at least 30 days' notice.

Payment and default

You authorise us or our payment provider to charge applicable fees. Failure to pay may result in suspension or termination after notice.

Disputed invoices must be raised in writing within 14 days of the invoice date with reasonable detail.

Trials and free tiers

Trials or promotional access may be withdrawn or converted to paid plans. Data handling during trials is subject to the Privacy Policy and DPA.

Acceptable Use

Your obligations

You agree to use [Product Name] lawfully and only for legitimate business or personal productivity purposes. You must not:

Violate UK GDPR, the DPA 2018, PECR, or other applicable law

Upload malware, conduct unauthorised penetration testing, or interfere with the Service

Attempt to access another tenant's data or bypass security controls

Use [Product Name] for spam, harassment, hate speech, or illegal content

Process special category data for clinical purposes or use [Product Name] as a medical record

Reverse engineer the Service except where law permits

Resell or sublicense the Service without our written consent

Abuse AI features (prompt injection, excessive automated calls, circumvention of rate limits)

Infringe intellectual property or privacy rights of others

Enforcement

We may investigate violations and suspend or terminate access where we reasonably believe you have breached these Terms or pose a security risk. We may report unlawful activity to authorities.

Content and Intellectual Property

Your content

You retain ownership of content you upload to [Product Name] ("Customer Content").

You grant [Company Name] a limited licence to host, process, transmit, back up, and display Customer Content solely to provide and improve the Service, comply with law, and as described in the Privacy Policy and DPA.

For AI features, you grant permission to process relevant content in-memory for the requested feature; we do not use Customer Content to train public foundation models.

Our IP

[Company Name] and its licensors own all rights in [Product Name], software, documentation, branding, and templates. Except for the limited right to use the Service during your subscription, no rights are granted to you.

Feedback may be used by us without obligation, provided we do not identify you without consent where required.

Copyright complaints

If you believe content on [Product Name] infringes copyright, contact legal@[company-domain] with sufficient detail for us to assess and respond.

Artificial Intelligence Features

AI terms

Optional AI features use Microsoft Azure OpenAI in the UK. See the AI Transparency Statement and AI Acceptable Use Policy.

AI output may be incorrect, incomplete, or biased — you must review before reliance

We do not guarantee accuracy, fitness for a particular purpose, or regulatory compliance of AI output

Prompts and raw completions are not stored; derived outputs you save are Customer Content

We may apply rate limits and safety filters; abuse may lead to suspension

Organisation admins may disable AI features where your plan allows

No automated legal or clinical decisions

[Product Name] AI does not make solely automated decisions producing legal or similarly significant effects. Human review is required before acting on AI suggestions in regulated contexts.

Data Protection and Confidentiality

Personal data

Each party complies with UK GDPR and the DPA 2018. Roles (controller/processor) are described in the Privacy Policy and DPA.

[Company Name] implements appropriate technical and organisational measures as described in our security documentation and DPA Annex.

You are responsible for lawful instructions to us as processor and for your users' use of [Product Name].

Security

We maintain safeguards including encryption, access controls, tenant isolation, and audit logging. No method of transmission over the Internet is 100% secure; you use the Service at your own risk within that limitation.

Confidentiality

Each party may receive confidential information from the other. The receiving party will protect it with reasonable care and use it only for the purpose of the agreement, except where disclosure is required by law or to professional advisers under confidentiality obligations.

Warranties and Disclaimers

Business users

To the extent permitted by law, we warrant that the Service will materially conform to the documentation in your Order during the subscription term. Remedy for breach: use reasonable efforts to correct or, if not cured within 30 days, allow termination and refund of prepaid fees for the unused portion.

Except as stated, the Service is provided "as is" and "as available".

Consumer notice

If you are a consumer, nothing in these Terms excludes rights that cannot be excluded under the Consumer Rights Act 2015. Statutory remedies may apply where the Service is faulty or not as described.

Disclaimer

We disclaim all other warranties, express or implied, including merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement, except where unlawful to exclude.

Limitation of Liability

Exclusions

Neither party excludes or limits liability for death or personal injury caused by negligence, fraud, or any liability that cannot be limited under applicable law.

Cap

Subject to the above, each party's total aggregate liability arising out of or related to these Terms or the Service in any 12-month period is limited to the greater of: (a) fees paid by you to us in that period, or (b) £10,000.

Neither party is liable for indirect, consequential, loss of profits, loss of data (except as recoverable under the DPA), or punitive damages, even if advised of the possibility.

AI and content

We are not liable for decisions you make based on AI output or Customer Content you or your users create.

Indemnity

By you

You will indemnify [Company Name] against claims, losses, and reasonable costs arising from: (a) your Customer Content or use of [Product Name] in breach of these Terms; (b) your violation of law or third-party rights; or (c) instructions to us that cause us to breach law, except where caused by our failure to follow documented instructions.

By us

We will indemnify you against third-party claims that the Service infringes UK intellectual property rights, provided you notify us promptly, allow us to control defence, and do not admit liability without consent. Our remedy may include modifying or replacing the Service or terminating with refund of prepaid unused fees.

Term, Suspension, and Termination

Term

These Terms commence when you accept them and continue for your subscription period, including renewals, until terminated.

Suspension

We may suspend access immediately for security risks, non-payment, suspected breach, or legal requirement. We will use reasonable efforts to notify you.

Termination

Either party may terminate for material breach not cured within 30 days of written notice.

You may cancel per your Order. We may terminate for convenience on standard plans with 30 days' notice.

On termination, your right to access ends. Data export may be available for a limited period; thereafter deletion applies per the Retention & Deletion Policy and DPA.

Survival

Provisions that by nature survive (fees owed, liability limits, confidentiality, IP, governing law) survive termination.

Force Majeure

Events

Neither party is liable for delay or failure due to events beyond reasonable control (e.g. natural disaster, war, terrorism, pandemic, government action, widespread outage of public networks or cloud providers), provided the affected party notifies the other and uses reasonable efforts to resume performance.

General Legal Terms

Assignment

You may not assign these Terms without our written consent. We may assign to an affiliate or in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets, with notice where required.

Notices

Notices to [Company Name]: legal@[company-domain] (contractual) and support@[company-domain] (operational).

Notices to you: email to your registered address or in-app notification.

Notices are deemed received on the next business day if sent by email before 5pm UK time.

Governing law

These Terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.

The courts of England and Wales have exclusive jurisdiction, except that either party may seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.

Severability and waiver

If any provision is unenforceable, the remainder stays in effect. Failure to enforce a provision is not a waiver.

No third party has rights under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999 except [Company Name] affiliates expressly stated.

Entire agreement

These Terms, the Order, Privacy Policy, Cookie Policy, and DPA constitute the entire agreement regarding the Service and supersede prior discussions, except for fraud or misrepresentation.

Changes

We may update these Terms. Material changes will be notified at least 30 days before they take effect (email or in-app). Continued use after the effective date constitutes acceptance. If you object, you may terminate before the effective date.

Contact

[Company Name]

Legal: legal@[company-domain]

Support: support@[company-domain]

Privacy: privacy@[company-domain]

Website: https://www.[company-domain]

[Product Name]: https://app.[company-domain]
